thread.title: Medvedev: “Russia Does Not Recognize the International Criminal Court, It Is Illegitimate”
thread.title_full: Medvedev: “Russia Does Not Recognize the International Criminal Court, It Is Illegitimate”

author: Davide Traglia
title: Medvedev: “Russia Does Not Recognize the International Criminal Court, It Is Illegitimate”
text:
Russia does not recognize the International Criminal Court (ICC) and considers it an illegitimate institution. This was stated Dmitry Medvedev, Deputy Chairman of the Security Council of the Russian Federation, commenting on the arrest warrants issued by the ICC against the Israeli President Benjamin Netanyahu and the former Minister of Defense Yoav Gallant.
The International Criminal Court has issued arrest warrants for Netanyahu, Gallant and Hamas military commander Mohammed Deif, accusing them of war crimes and crimes against humanity. The Israeli prime minister's office has responded harshly, accusing the ICC of trying to isolate Israel and supporting terrorism against the Jewish state. "Russia does not recognize the jurisdiction of the International Criminal Court and considers it a politically motivated body without legitimacy," Medvedev was quoted by the Russian news agency "Sputnik", reiterating Moscow's historic position towards the ICC.
